nodes.label: Airport Self-Service Kiosk
nodes.notes: Req 9.9: POI device tamper protection and periodic inspections required. Kiosk hardware must be PTS-validated. Physical security controls mandatory (locked enclosure, CCTV). Inspect for skimming overlays.

nodes.label: Onboard POS (Cabin Crew)
nodes.notes: Req 9.9: Mobile POS device inventory must be maintained. Inspect devices for tampering before each flight. Cellular or satellite link used for transmission — TLS 1.2+ mandatory (Req 4.2.1). Device must be PA-DSS validated or assessed application.

nodes.label: Passenger Browser
nodes.notes: Customer-facing checkout. Req 4.2.1: TLS 1.2+ enforced. No PAN stored in browser cache, cookies or local storage. Implement CSP and SRI headers to prevent JavaScript skimming (Magecart). Consider hosted-fields or iFrame redirect to reduce scope.

nodes.label: WFH Agent PC / Softphone
nodes.notes: Req 1 / 12.5: Agent workstation is in-scope CDE even though agent never hears PAN (DTMF capture). Full endpoint controls required: EDR, host FW, FDE, MFA, no split-tunnel VPN. Physical workspace policy must be documented and annually acknowledged.

nodes.label: Kiosk P2PE Module
nodes.notes: PCI P2PE-listed hardware security module embedded in kiosk. Encrypts PAN at point of interaction using DUKPT before any data leaves the secure crypto boundary. Kiosk host OS and network are OUT OF SCOPE for PAN — P2PE listing must be verified annually with P2PE-SP.

nodes.label: Airline DMZ Firewall
nodes.notes: Req 1: Default-deny inbound and outbound. All rules documented with business justification. Review every 6 months. Segments CDE from corporate LAN, crew Wi-Fi, and third-party networks. Stateful inspection. Separate rule sets for kiosk P2PE and onboard POS.

nodes.label: WAF / CDN (Edge)
nodes.notes: Req 6.4: WAF required for all internet-facing web applications. Block OWASP Top 10, enforce TLS 1.2+, enable bot protection and rate limiting. CDN provides DDoS mitigation and TLS offload at edge.

nodes.label: Amazon Connect (AWS Cloud)
nodes.notes: PCI-compliant cloud contact centre (AWS manages underlying infra). DTMF digits captured by Connect IVR flow — plaintext PAN is never streamed to agent audio. CRITICAL: Call recording must be PAUSED before customer is prompted for card number (use SetRecordingBehavior block — PAUSE). Req 4.2.1: All API calls to payment gateway use TLS 1.2+. Confirm AWS Shared Responsibility scope in BAA / compliance documentation.

nodes.label: IDS / IPS
nodes.notes: Req 11.5: Network-based intrusion detection on all CDE ingress/egress. File-integrity monitoring (FIM) on CDE servers. Alert on anomalous PAN movement, unexpected outbound connections, and brute-force attempts. Logs forwarded to centralised SIEM.

nodes.label: Airline Booking Engine
nodes.notes: Req 6: Secure SDLC including static/dynamic analysis and annual penetration test. PAN must not appear in HTTP logs, URLs, error messages or debug output. Consider hosted payment fields to push card capture scope to gateway provider.

nodes.label: Agent CRM Desktop
nodes.notes: Req 7 / 8: Role-based access control, MFA for all CDE access. Agents see masked PAN only (BIN + last 4). Full PAN display requires elevated privilege with audit log (Req 3.3.1). Req 9: Full-disk encryption on agent laptop, MDM-managed, clean-desk policy for WFH.

nodes.label: Call Recordings (Amazon S3)
nodes.notes: Req 3 / 10: Recordings stored encrypted at rest in S3 (SSE-KMS, CMK managed in AWS KMS). Recording MUST be paused before IVR prompts for card number and resumed after. Access to recordings restricted by IAM policy and logged (Req 10). Retention policy: minimum 12 months with 3 months immediately accessible. PAN must never appear in any recording — verify with quarterly sampling.

nodes.label: Tokenization Vault
nodes.notes: Issues surrogate tokens for all stored card references (recurring fares, saved cards, refunds). PAN held only inside vault boundary. Vault dramatically reduces CDE scope for merchant-side systems. Confirm vault is PCI DSS assessed and token format does not preserve PAN structure.

nodes.label: Airline Payment Hub
nodes.notes: Central payment gateway aggregating all four channel flows. All inbound traffic arrives encrypted. Handles auth routing, tokenisation requests, settlement batching and 3DS2 for e-commerce. Must itself be PCI DSS Level 1 certified. Shared responsibility matrix required for outsourced gateway.

nodes.label: HSM
nodes.notes: Req 3.7: FIPS 140-2 Level 3 validated hardware security module. Manages all CDE cryptographic keys — key generation, storage, distribution and destruction. Key custodian split-knowledge procedures documented. Annual key-ceremony audit trail retained.

nodes.label: Token Store (DB)
nodes.notes: Req 3.3: Stores tokens only — no PANs at rest in this database. If raw PAN retention is required for chargebacks, apply strong encryption (AES-256) with keys managed in HSM (Req 3.5). Data retention schedule enforced: purge expired records per policy. Quarterly data-discovery scans to verify no unencrypted PAN (Req 12.3).

nodes.label: Card Network (Visa / MC)
nodes.notes: External — out of airline CDE scope. Receives auth requests from payment hub. Returns authorisation codes. Network-level segmentation must prevent any direct access from non-payment systems to card network connections.

nodes.label: Acquiring Bank
nodes.notes: External — out of airline CDE scope. Receives daily settlement batches from Payment Hub. Funds credited to airline merchant account. Acquirer relationship defines merchant PCI DSS level and assessment obligations (SAQ vs ROC).
